title: MongoDB Replica Set Member State Degradation
app: mongosh scripted input, MongoDB Atlas metrics API forwarded to Splunk, or DB Connect export
spl:
index=database sourcetype="mongodb:rs_status"
| where stateStr IN ("STARTUP","STARTUP2","RECOVERING","ROLLBACK","DOWN","UNKNOWN") OR health==0 OR health=="0"
| stats latest(stateStr) as state latest(health) as health by name, set_name, host
| sort state
description: Replica set members not in PRIMARY or SECONDARY healthy states indicate elections, sync loss, or network partitions—core MongoDB availability monitoring.
value: Shortens downtime by detecting bad member states before application drivers exhaust their server selection timeouts.
implementation: Poll rs.status() every 30–60s from a monitoring user. Normalize stateStr and health booleans. Suppress alerts during planned step-downs using change calendar. Track optime lag between primary and secondaries in a companion panel.
visualization: Table (member, state, health), Timeline (state changes), Single value (degraded members).
